ecarx.carext.storage

Interface ISecureStorage


  • public interface ISecureStorage
    This class provides the primary API for accessing TEE secure storage.
    Since:
    12

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Default Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      default int closeSecureStorage(int fd) 
      default int deleteSecureStorage(java.lang.String name) 
      default int openSecureStorage(java.lang.String name, int flag) 
      default int readSecureStorage(int fd, int count, byte[] buf) 
      default int resetSecureStorage(int flag) 
      default int seekSecureStorage(int fd, long offset, int whence) 
      default int writeSecureStorage(int fd, byte[] buf, int count) 

    • Method Detail

      • openSecureStorage

        default int openSecureStorage(java.lang.String name,
                              int flag)
                       throws java.io.IOException
        Parameters:
        name - The name of the secure storage to open.
        flag - The mode in which to open the secure storage. This should be one of the following constants: - SS_RD: Read-only mode. - SS_WR: Write-only mode. - SS_RDWR: Read-write mode. And can optionally include one or more of the following constants: - SS_SHARE: If you need to globally share a file, you need to add the SS_SHARE flag when creating and reading/writing the file. - SS_CREATE: If the specified file does not exist, create it. - SS_APPEND: Each write operation is appended to the end of the file. - SS_PERMANENT: The file is permanently stored and will not be deleted. This flag needs to be used with SS_CREATE.
        Returns:
        On success, returns a non-negative integer representing the secure storage handle. On failure, returns a negative error code.
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • closeSecureStorage

        default int closeSecureStorage(int fd)
                        throws java.io.IOException
        Parameters:
        fd - The file descriptor of the secure storage to close. This should be a value that was returned by secure_storage_open or secure_storage_open2.
        Returns:
        On success, returns 0. On failure, returns a negative error code.
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • readSecureStorage

        default int readSecureStorage(int fd,
                              int count,
                              byte[] buf)
                       throws java.io.IOException
        Parameters:
        fd - The file descriptor of the secure storage to read from. This should be a value that was returned by secure_storage_open or secure_storage_open2.
        buf - The read data buffer
        count - The maximum number of bytes to be read.
        Returns:
        On success, the number of bytes read is returned (zero indicates end of file). On failure, a negative error code is returned.
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • writeSecureStorage

        default int writeSecureStorage(int fd,
                               byte[] buf,
                               int count)
                        throws java.io.IOException
        Parameters:
        fd - The file descriptor of the secure storage to write to. This should be a value that was returned by secure_storage_open or secure_storage_open2.
        buf - The buffer containing the data to be written.
        count - The maximum number of bytes to be written.
        Returns:
        On success, the number of bytes written is returned (zero indicates nothing was written). On failure, a negative error code is returned.
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • seekSecureStorage

        default int seekSecureStorage(int fd,
                              long offset,
                              int whence)
                       throws java.io.IOException
        Parameters:
        fd - The file descriptor of the secure storage. This should be a value that was returned by secure_storage_open or secure_storage_open2.
        offset - The number of bytes to offset from 'whence'.
        whence - The position from where to offset. This should be one of the following constants: - SEEK_SET: The offset is set to 'offset' bytes. - SEEK_CUR: The offset is set to its current location plus 'offset' bytes. - SEEK_END: The offset is set to the size of the secure storage plus 'offset' bytes.
        Returns:
        On success, the resulting offset location as measured in bytes from the beginning of the secure storage is returned. On failure, a negative error code is returned.
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • deleteSecureStorage

        default int deleteSecureStorage(java.lang.String name)
                         throws java.io.IOException
        Parameters:
        name - The name of the secure storage to delete.
        Returns:
        On success, returns 0. On failure, returns a negative error code.
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • resetSecureStorage

        default int resetSecureStorage(int flag)
                        throws java.io.IOException
        Returns:
        On success, returns 0. On failure, returns a negative error code.
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException
